commands.author: solodmd
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ec3fe595fb6ffef7c1d6932dc5b8e347f5617a8d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram and Discord preview streaming so live previews do not inherit global block delivery, and changes agent commentary capture so hidden progress is not rendered as answer text.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +402. Total +484 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: selected-quote Telegram replies make canStreamAnswerDraft false, but the PR still forces disableBlockStreaming true when preview mode suppresses accountBlockStreamingEnabled. The commentary gap is also source-reproducible because the follow-up runner still gates onCommentaryText on commentaryProgressEnabled.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer fixes for the review findings plus current-head proof; the insufficient proof gate makes this human-review work rather than a ClawSweeper repair/automerge candidate.

target.title: fix(browser): truncate element text on code-point boundaries to avoid lone surrogates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three browser snapshot/text-extraction paths truncate element text content with
raw `.slice(0, N)`. When an emoji or other astral character straddles the cut
point, the raw slice keeps only the high-surrogate half, producing a lone
`\uD83D` ‚ÄĒ malformed UTF-16.

Affected paths:
- `cdp.ts`: `findCursorInteractiveElements` truncates `textContent` at 100
- `pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts`: `collectSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120
- `routes/agent.snapshot.ts`: `collectChromeMcpSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120

## Why This Change Was Made

Since the affected code runs in the browser page context (CDP `Runtime.evaluate`
/ Playwright `page.evaluate`), the SDK's `truncateUtf16Safe` is not importable.
Instead, `.replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")` is appended after each `.slice()`
to strip any dangling high surrogate at the cut boundary. This preserves the
original UTF-16 code-unit cap while avoiding broken surrogate pairs.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change. Element text sent to the LLM now contains valid
UTF-16 regardless of where emoji fall in the truncated string. The code-unit
caps (100 / 120) remain unchanged.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm test extensions/browser` ‚ÄĒ 59 tests passed

### Real behavior proof

The proof script launches a headless Chrome instance, connects via CDP, injects
a test page with emoji at the truncation boundaries, and evaluates the **exact
same** truncation expression used in the three changed paths:

```
‚úÖ Chrome started

‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Testing cdp.ts path (findCursorInteractiveElements) ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
Pattern: String(el.textContent || "").replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim().slice(0, 100).replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")

‚úÖ ASCII text within limit
   original: 11 units
   AFTER  fix: slice(0,100).replace() ‚Üí 11 units, dangling: false ‚úÖ
‚úÖ Long ASCII truncated
   original: 200 units
   AFTER  fix: slice(0,100).replace() ‚Üí 100 units, dangling: false ‚úÖ
‚úÖ 99x + ūüėÄ + tail (emoji at boundary)
   original: 105 units
   ‚ö†ÔłŹ  BEFORE fix: slice(0,100) ‚Üí ends with dangling surrogate (tail: 0x78 0x78 0xd83d)
   AFTER  fix: slice(0,100).replace() ‚Üí 99 units, dangling: false ‚úÖ
‚úÖ 99x + ūüéČūüéČ + tail (multiple emoji)
   original: 107 units
   ‚ö†ÔłŹ  BEFORE fix: slice(0,100) ‚Üí ends with dangling surrogate (tail: 0x78 0x78 0xd83c)
   AFTER  fix: slice(0,100).replace() ‚Üí 99 units, dangling: false ‚úÖ
‚úÖ 51x + 25ūüėÄ + tail (code-unit cap)
   original: 105 units
   ‚ö†ÔłŹ  BEFORE fix: slice(0,100) ‚Üí ends with dangling surrogate (tail: 0xd83d 0xde00 0xd83d)
   AFTER  fix: slice(0,100).replace() ‚Üí 99 units, dangling: false ‚úÖ
‚úÖ 50x + ūüėÄ + 48y (complete pair kept)
   original: 100 units
   AFTER  fix: slice(0,100).replace() ‚Üí 100 units, dangling: false ‚úÖ

ūüďä 6/6 CDP path tests passed

‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Testing snapshot URL path (pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts:80) ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ
Pattern: .slice(0, 120).replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")

‚úÖ anchor a1: before dangling=true ‚Üí after dangling=false
‚úÖ anchor a2: before dangling=true ‚Üí after dangling=false
‚úÖ anchor a3: before dangling=false ‚Üí after dangling=false

ūüďä 3/3 snapshot path tests passed

==================================================
Total: 9/9 tests passed
All outputs free of dangling surrogates: ‚úÖ
```

Each test exercises the actual OpenClaw code path expression against a real
browser page. The before-fix output shows dangling high surrogates (0xD83D,
0xD83C) that would be sent to the LLM; the after-fix output is clean.

Reproduce:
```bash
node --import tsx proof-browser-openclaw-path.mts
```

## Tests

`extensions/browser/src/browser/browser-text-truncation.test.ts` ‚ÄĒ 6 new tests
covering ASCII pass-through, long ASCII truncation, dangling-surrogate removal,
multiple emoji, code-unit cap preservation, and complete-pair pass-through.
